response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirement: deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015; his career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms. He shows no exposure to IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, or OT/industrial protocols, which are central to this position.
strengths: Proven experience building high-throughput, low-latency systems (async I/O, fibers, caching at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and a track record of mentoring via workshops and courses
Familiarity with advantageous stack items: Node.js, React, Docker, Linux, and event-driven architecture (GISCollective)
concerns: Minimal and outdated professional C++ experience (brief 2014-2015 role plus a thesis project), against a role demanding expert-level C++/STL
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or industrial protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not embedded/industrial platform development or architecture leadership in that domain
